latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0665W
Gattaca PLC
11 March 2026
11 March 2026
Gattaca plc
("
Gattaca
" or the "
Group
")
Notice of Results and Investor Presentations
Gattaca plc, the specialist staffing solutions business, will announce its interim results for the six months to 31 January 2026 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.
Investor Meet Company
Management will provide a live presentation relating to the Interim Results via Investor Meet Company at 09:30 GMT on Tuesday 24 March 2026.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders. Questions can be submitted pre-event via your Investor Meet Company dashboard up until 23 March 2026, 09:00 GMT, or at any time during the live presentation.
Investors can sign up to Investor Meet Company for free and add to meet GATTACA PLC via:
https://www.investormeetcompany.com/gattaca-plc/register-investor
ShareSoc
The Company will also be holding a live Investor presentation and Q&A session via the ShareSoc Growth Company Seminar (Hybrid Event) Investor Platform on Wednesday 25 March 2026 at 5pm UK local time. This event will consist of an in-person seminar in London, which will also be streamed live (and recorded), allowing members to watch virtually.
The presentation is open to all existing and potential shareholders and attendees can register and attend using the following ShareSoc link:
https://www.sharesoc.org/sharesoc-growth-company-seminar-hybrid-event-25-march-2026-5pm/
For further information please contact:
Gattaca plc

latest.title: Trading Update
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4960S
Gattaca PLC
11 February 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION FOR THE PURPOSES OF ARTICLE 7 OF THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION (EU) 596/2014 AS IT FORMS PART OF UK DOMESTIC LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 ("MAR"), AND IS DISCLOSED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE COMPANY'S OBLIGATIONS UNDER ARTICLE 17 OF MAR.
11 February 2026
Gattaca plc
("
Gattaca
" or the "
Group
")
Trading Update
Strong H1 NFI performance trading ahead of expectations
Gattaca plc, the specialist staffing solutions business, is pleased to provide the following trading update for the six months ended 31 January 2026 ("H1 26").
·
Total Group NFI is expected to be £21.2m (H1 25: £18.9m). On a like-for-like basis² ("LFL"), NFI increased by 7% YoY to £20.2m, driven by strong contract performance across core growth sectors.
·
LFL contract NFI increased by 13% YoY, reflecting increased activity levels in our core sectors.
·
Permanent fees were stable with LFL permanent NFI up 2% YoY.
·
LFL Statement of Work ("SoW") NFI was down due to timing delays on major client programmes, with activity expected to remain subdued in the second half of the year.
·
Total Group sales headcount at 31 January 2026 reduced by 6% YoY. The Group continued to focus on operational efficiency and disciplined resource allocation, while maintaining investment in target sectors. Sales-to-Support mix remained at 71:29 (31 January 2025: 71:29).
·
The Group expects to report statutory net cash as at 31 January 2026 of £13.0m (31 January 2025: net cash of £16.8m). Reduction in net cash during period reflects:
- Working capital absorption as contractors numbers have increased;
- Consideration paid for InfoSec acquisition; and
- Final dividend payment in respect of FY25.
Outlook
·
Group guidance for FY26 adjusted profit before tax expected to be £4.5m.
·
The Group's ambition for FY26 remains to invest in identified markets, adding capable experienced sales consultants and growing our sales headcount by 10%.
·
The Group expects to announce an interim dividend at its Interim Results.
Notice of Interim Results
The Group will announce its results for the six months to 31 January 2026 on Tuesday 24 March 2026.
Matthew Wragg, Chief Executive Officer said:
"I am pleased to see our positive momentum continue to build and to report that the Group is trading ahead of expectations. Our strategic investments in growth opportunities are delivering and we have the majority of our sectors experiencing year on year growth.
The InfoSec team have performed well and enhanced the Group's cyber capability contributing positively to contract momentum during the period and was fully integrated in the Group's systems during H1.
With a strengthened technology platform, growing customer base and improving operational efficiency, I remain confident in the Group's ability to deliver sustainable growth
."
1.
NFI is calculated as revenue less contractor payroll costs.
2. On 4 August 2025, the Group acquired the entire issued share capital of InfoSec People Limited, a specialist cyber security recruitment consultancy company based in the UK. The total consideration was £2.1m, comprising an initial payment of £1.5m and deferred consideration of up to £0.6m, payable over the next four years and subject to performance criteria being met by InfoSec People.
For further information please contact:
Gattaca plc
Matthew Wragg, Chief Executive Officer
Oliver Whittaker, Chief Financial Officer

latest.title: Grant of Options and Director/PDMR dealing
latest.content:
19 Dec 2025 15:29
RNS Number : 3492M
Gattaca PLC
19 December 2025
19 December 2025
Gattaca plc
("Gattaca" or the "Company")
Grant of Options and Director/PDMR dealing
Gattaca, the specialist staffing solutions business, announces the following conditional share awards over ordinary shares in Gattaca were granted to the Company's PDMRs under the Matchtech Group Plc 2016 Long Term Incentive Plan (the "LTIP"):
Executive Directors
Title
Number of Shares
Matthew Wragg
Chief Executive Officer
270,000
Oliver Whittaker
Chief Financial Officer
206,000
The LTIP award is a conditional grant of shares, which vest and become exercisable at nil-cost on the third anniversary of the Grant Date, subject to the individual's continued employment with the Group. Vesting of the LTIP awards are subject to the achievement of earnings per share targets (the "EPS Targets") over the year ended 31 July 2028 and total shareholder performance (the "TSR Targets") over the three-year period to 31 July 2028; with 25% of the maximum award vesting for threshold performance in both cases.
For Executive Directors, 50% of the award is subject to the achievement of EPS Targets and 35% is subject to the achievement of TSR Targets, with the balance being subject to the rate of all staff attrition and gender diversity amongst leadership and management.
Further, Matthew Wragg and Oliver Whittaker, on 16 December 2025, each sold 20,000 Ordinary Shares to assist satisfying tax liabilities resulting from the exercise options on 16 December 2025.
The below notification, made in accordance with the requirements of the EU Market Abuse Regulation, gives further details of the transaction on 10 December 2025.

company_30s.what_they_do: Gattaca plc is a specialist staffing and recruitment firm that places skilled professionals in the UK’s technology, engineering and life‑sciences sectors. They operate a network of consultants who match candidates to roles, earning fees from both clients and candidates.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The sectors Gattaca serves are high‑growth and labour‑intensive. Their niche focus on tech and life‑sciences gives them an edge over generic recruitment agencies, allowing them to command higher fees and secure repeat business from companies that need specialised talent.
company_30s.current_state: The company is in a period of restructuring and shareholder communication. Recent moves include a share‑buyback program and a series of quarterly updates, indicating an attempt to stabilise cash flow and signal confidence to investors.

rns_narrative: Gattaca has been busy keeping shareholders in the loop: director changes, option exercises, and a share‑buyback program show the board is actively managing capital structure.
Quarterly results and investor presentations have been issued, suggesting the company is preparing to disclose performance data that could influence valuation.
The focus on shareholder returns (dividends, buybacks) points to a strategy of creating value for investors rather than aggressive expansion.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Silence with a few cautious nods – the community is largely indifferent, no buzz or hype.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish chatter is visible; the few supportive posts highlight the company’s cash position but don’t signal a buying rally.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A handful of traders voice concerns about past tipster losses and question whether Gattaca’s recent earnings justify its share price.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment remains flat; there is no noticeable swing toward optimism or pessimism.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for Gattaca is flat, meaning the market is not actively searching for the name – a perfect backdrop for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: The upcoming trading update on 11 Feb 2026 could reveal earnings momentum or a new contract pipeline.
The share‑buyback program, if it continues, will reduce supply and support the price.
A strong quarterly result or a new client acquisition announced during the investor presentation would create a positive narrative.
Technical support at the 114p level (previous target) could act as a floor if the price starts to climb.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike coupled with a break above the 114p support. Also monitor the share‑buyback schedule – if the buyback accelerates, it signals confidence from insiders.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stages of a recovery, having already hit a deep trough. A clear sign of mean reversion will be a sustained bounce off the 114p level, followed by higher highs.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Gattaca has suffered a deep price collapse, leaving a large upside if the market recognises its cash cushion and niche advantage. If the price rebounds to the 114p support and then to 152p, the upside from the current trading level could be more than 100%. The deep crash aligns with the highest‑reward signal colour in the model, suggesting a strong potential for a breakout.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear earnings beat or new contract, the price could stay stuck. Insider selling or a slowdown in the tech/recruitment market could keep the valuation low. The worst case is a further slide if the buyback stalls.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss is limited to the current price (near zero in the data). Upside to 114p represents a 200‑plus percent gain, giving a risk‑reward ratio far above 1:1.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce off the 114p support with a small position (e.g., 5% of capital). Place a stop just below 100p to protect against a false break. Use a trailing stop to lock profits as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the 11 Feb 2026 update. If earnings beat expectations or a new contract is announced, consider a quick entry at 110p with a tight stop at 100p.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk is evident; the company is not delisting or bankrupting. The only real risk is a lack of catalyst, so the position can stay open until a clear signal appears.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Gattaca sits on a deep crash, has no current catalysts but a potential buy‑back and earnings update that could trigger a mean‑reversion rally, offering a highly asymmetric upside if the price clears the 114p support.
